title: [FIRRTL] IMCP Tries to Drive Invalid Value
body:
#4445 introduced a bug where invalid can sometimes show up as the destination of a connect. 

Consider:

```mlir
firrtl.circuit "Foo"  {
  firrtl.module @Foo(in %clock: !firrtl.clock) {
    %a = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint<1>
    %r = firrtl.reg interesting_name %clock : !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.strictconnect %r, %a : !firrtl.uint<1>
  }
}
```

Running this through IMCP (`circt-opt -firrtl-imconstprop Foo.mlir`) produces:

```
Tmp.mlir:5:5: error: connect has invalid flow: the destination expression has source flow, expected sink or duplex flow
    firrtl.strictconnect %r, %a : !firrtl.uint<1>
    ^
Tmp.mlir:4:10: note: the destination was defined here
    %r = firrtl.reg interesting_name %clock : !firrtl.uint<1>
         ^
```

With the final illegal MLIR:

```mlir
"firrtl.circuit"() ({
  "firrtl.module"() ({
  ^bb0(%arg0: !firrtl.clock):
    %0 = "firrtl.wire"() {annotations = [], name = "a", nameKind = #firrtl<name_kind droppable_name>} : () -> !firrtl.uint<1>
    %1 = "firrtl.invalidvalue"() : () -> !firrtl.uint<1>
    %2 = "firrtl.reg"(%arg0) {annotations = [], name = "r", nameKind = #firrtl<name_kind interesting_name>} : (!firrtl.clock) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
    "firrtl.strictconnect"(%1, %0) : (!firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>) -> ()
  }) {annotations = [], parameters = [], portAnnotations = [], portDirections = false, portNames = ["clock"], portSyms = [], portTypes = [!firrtl.clock], sym_name = "Foo"} : () -> ()
}) {annotations = [], name = "Foo"} : () -> ()
```

This is a heavily reduced example derived from an internal design. The internal design showed this error only if compiled with `-O=debug`. The error was not present with `-O=release`.

_Edit: this fixes the internal error I observed._
